question_text: Explain why melting ice requires energy but freezing water releases energy.
correct_answer: Melting requires energy to break apart the fixed structure of ice so particles can move more freely. Freezing releases energy as particles slow down and form a rigid structure. Both are opposite processes with opposite energy changes.
explanation: Energy is needed to overcome forces between particles during melting, but energy is released when those forces reform during freezing. This demonstrates that reversible physical changes are accompanied by energy transfers in opposite directions.
